title: 'std::out_of_range' at 'dict::at()` during techmap with shift logic loop
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue
Run `yosys -p "opt_clean; techmap" top.v` with what is an obviously badly written piece of Verilog:

```verilog
// top.v
module top(output x);
        assign x = 1 >> x;
endmodule
```

## Expected behavior
Yosys reports an error due to the logic loop, rather than crashing.

## Actual behavior
An `std::out_of_range` is raised during the techmap pass.

```
3.2. Continuing TECHMAP pass.
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::out_of_range'
  what():  dict::at()
Aborted (core dumped)
```

(thank you to @Ravenslofty for helping minimise)
